message.content: Adversaries may exploit system services to execute commands or programs, either locally or remotely. This technique can be used to run malicious content by interacting with or creating services. Services often run at boot, aiding in persistence, but can also be used for temporary execution. For instance, TeamTNT has created system services to deploy cryptocurrency mining software. The potential impact includes unauthorized execution of malicious code, persistence mechanisms, and resource hijacking. Common exploitation methods involve creating or modifying system services to run malicious payloads. Mitigation strategies include monitoring and restricting service creation and modification, employing application whitelisting, and ensuring proper configuration and patch management to prevent unauthorized service manipulation.
